seo_title: Teaching AI models to say 'I'm not sure': New method improves…
meta_description: A new training method improves the reliability of AI confidence estimates without sacrificing performance, addressing a root cause of hallucination in…
body_html:
A new training method improves the reliability of AI confidence estimates without sacrificing performance, addressing a root cause of hallucination in reasoning models.

Key Findings

  • The technique, called RLCR (Reinforcement Learning with Calibration Rewards), trains language models to produce calibrated confidence estimates alongside their answers.
  • In experiments across multiple benchmarks, RLCR reduced calibration error by up to 90 percent while maintaining or improving accuracy.
  • The work will be presented at the International Conference on Learning Representations later this month.

Root Cause of Overconfidence

Source Description
Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods Reward models for getting the right answer, and penalize them for getting it wrong.
Training Approach Used in Systems like OpenAI's o1 The same reward for correct answers as for guessing correctly by chance.

Benchmarks and Results

  • In addition to coming up with an answer, the model thinks about its uncertainty in that answer, and outputs a confidence score.
  • The calibration error was reduced by up to 90 percent while maintaining or improving accuracy.

Conclusion

The new training method addresses a root cause of hallucination in reasoning models, improving the reliability of confidence estimates without sacrificing performance.
